basis.claim: Code review should examine design, functionality, complexity, tests, naming, comments, style, documentation, every line and broader context.
Secure development should reduce vulnerabilities, mitigate exploitation impact and address root causes to prevent recurrence.
Secure coding requires validation, access control, error handling/logging, file management and general coding practices.

binding_rules.then: cdr_gate_result.verdict must be PASS before stage PASS
cdr_packet_path, cdr_gate_path, cdr_eval_path, and cdr_evidence_hashes are required
stage verdict must be BLOCKED_CDR_REQUIRED or FAIL_CDR
stage must explicitly report whether code-bearing work occurred and how CDR was invoked or why it was not applicable
they are code-bearing even if not executable source files

binding_rules.if: work is described as small/simple/just a script/just HTML/just a validator
binding_rules.then: no exemption is granted; CDR remains required if code-bearing

canonical_rule: CDR is a mandatory subgate of MPP for all code-bearing work.

definitions.cdr: Coding Done Right: mandatory code-acceptance and recursive improvement gate invoked by MPP for code-bearing work.
definitions.code_bearing_work: Any task that writes, patches, generates, audits, validates, exports, or operationally depends on executable or runtime-affecting artifacts, including Python, JavaScript, shell, Node, HTML/JS, validators, gates, schemas that drive execution, packaging scripts, and runtime policies.
definitions.mpp: Mandatory Process Pipeline: full governed execution workflow for MetaBlooms stages.

non_goals: Does not re-export full authority in this stage.
Does not rewrite all historic MPP artifacts; installs a forward-enforcing binding gate and invariant.
